topic: uncertainty estimation for reliable medical image classification

scope_classification.label: local_experiment
scope_classification.rationale: The topic can be explored with small simulations, synthetic data, or lightweight local experiments on the Mac.
scope_classification.feasibility_note: Yes, this research topic is feasible on your MacBook M4 with 36GB RAM and Apple MPS GPU, as uncertainty estimation methods like Monte Carlo dropout or ensembles don't require excessive computational resources. For datasets, I recommend CheXpert (chest X-rays), MURA (musculoskeletal radiographs), or NIH ChestX-ray14, all of which are publicly available and suitable for uncertainty estimation research. You can expect experiment times of approximately 2-4 hours for smaller datasets like MURA, 8-16

deep_read.paper_title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning

method.name: Reflexion
method.description: An alternative approach that uses verbal reinforcement to help agents learn from prior failings by converting binary or scalar feedback from the environment into verbal feedback in the form of a textual summary.
key_components: Converts binary or scalar feedback into verbal feedback
Adds verbal feedback as additional context for the LLM agent in the next episode
Acts as a 'semantic' gradient signal providing concrete directions for improvement
Stores natural language experience summaries in long-term memory
feedback_generation_methods: Pre-defined heuristics for common failure cases
Self-evaluation using binary classification with LLMs or self-written unit tests

advantages: Lightweight and doesn't require finetuning the LLM
Allows for more nuanced forms of feedback compared to scalar or vector rewards
Provides explicit and interpretable episodic memory over prior experiences
Provides more explicit hints for actions in future episodes
disadvantages: Relies on the power of the LLM's self-evaluation capabilities (or heuristics)

task_types: Decision-making tasks (sequential action choices over long trajectories)
Programming tasks (teaching agents to use external tools)

results.decision_making: 22% improvement on AlfWorld tasks over strong baseline approaches in 12 iterative learning steps
results.programming: Improvement mentioned but details cut off in text

deep_read.title: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback
deep_read.authors: Not specified in the provided text.
deep_read.summary: This paper introduces 'Self-Refine', an iterative self-refinement algorithm for Large Language Models (LLMs). The method enables LLMs to improve their own outputs by generating self-feedback and using it to refine a draft, without requiring additional training or human assistance. It uses few-shot prompting to guide the process and demonstrates superior performance on diverse generation tasks compared to direct generation.

methodology.core_concept: Iterative self-refinement, where an LLM creates an initial draft and refines it based on self-generated feedback, inspired by human problem-solving.
process: The model (ℳ) generates an initial output.
The model (ℳ) generates 'feedback' on its own output.
The model (ℳ) uses the feedback to 'refine' the previous output.
Steps 2 and 3 are repeated iteratively until a stopping condition is met (e.g., a fixed number of iterations or the model determines no further refinement is needed).

implementation_details.technique: Few-shot prompting is used to guide the model for both feedback generation and refinement.
implementation_details.model_used: The same underlying language model (ℳ) is used for both feedback and refinement.
implementation_details.instantiation: The method is instantiated with language models such as GPT-3.5.
implementation_details.supervision: Does not require additional training, domain-specific data, or human assistance.

methodology.problem_addressed: Existing iterative refinement methods often rely on training a separate model with domain-specific data, external supervision, or reward models, which can be expensive or infeasible to obtain.

key_contributions: Demonstrates that LLMs can perform iterative self-refinement without additional training.
Proposes the 'Self-Refine' algorithm that uses a single model for feedback and refinement.
Shows that this approach leads to higher-quality outputs on a wide range of tasks.

evaluation.tasks: 7 generation tasks spanning diverse domains, including natural language and source-code generation.

results.primary_finding: The 'Self-Refine' algorithm outperforms direct generation from strong LLMs like GPT-3.
results.scope: The result was observed across 7 diverse generation tasks.

paper_metadata.title: Aging States Estimation and Monitoring Strategies of Li-Ion Batteries Using Incremental Capacity Analysis and Gaussian Process Regression
paper_metadata.problem_statement: Accurate estimation of State of Health (SoH) and Remaining Useful Life (RUL) is essential for predictive maintenance and safety, but existing methods often require dense cycling histories which are not available in practical scenarios with only a few diagnostic cycles.
paper_metadata.objectives: To develop data-efficient approaches for estimating SoH and RUL from sparse measurements, specifically using Incremental Capacity Analysis and Gaussian Process Regression.

methods.name: Incremental Capacity Analysis (ICA)
methods.description: An indirect analysis method used for battery aging analysis.

methods.name: Gaussian Process Regression (GPR)
methods.description: A machine learning approach used for regression tasks, applied here for SoH and RUL estimation.

key_findings: Electro-chemical models are limited by extensive parametrization and computational effort.
Electric equivalent circuit models require large, high-resolution datasets for optimization.
Machine learning approaches, including GPR, are gaining attention for SoH and RUL estimation due to their ability to handle complex relationships.
A key challenge is mapping sparse raw measurement data to meaningful features describing battery aging.
challenges_addressed: Difficulty of applying methods that rely on dense cycling histories when only a few diagnostic cycles are available.
Need for data-efficient approaches to extract information from sparse measurements.
High computational cost and extensive parametrization requirements of some model-based methods (e.g., electro-chemical models).

deep_read.title: Aging States Estimation and Monitoring Strategies of Li-Ion Batteries Using Incremental Capacity Analysis and Gaussian Process Regression

introduction.background: Wind energy is a rapidly growing renewable energy source with unlimited availability and minimal environmental impact. In 2023, new global wind power capacity is expected to surpass 100 GW for the first time, with projections reaching 143 GW by the end of the decade.

introduction.importance: Effective wind speed modeling and prediction plays a crucial role in the overall wind power generation process.

parsed_papers.possible_gap: Based on full paper reading: See deep_read.json for details

deep_read.paper_title: Bayesian Uncertainty Quantification with Anchored Ensembles for Robust EV Power Consumption Prediction
deep_read.abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of driving-range uncertainty in Battery Electric Vehicles (BEVs) by developing a robust model for energy consumption prediction. Using real-world telematics data, the authors employ a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network to estimate instantaneous power consumption. The core contribution is a comprehensive Uncertainty Quantification (UQ) analysis that captures both aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty. They adopt a Bayesian ensembling method extended to LSTMs to model epistemic uncertainty and incorporate a probabilistic output layer to model aleatoric uncertainty, resulting in predictive intervals suitable for online range management and decision-making.
research_questions_or_objectives: To estimate instantaneous power consumption using an LSTM network.
To perform a comprehensive Uncertainty Quantification (UQ) analysis for EV power prediction.
To capture and model both aleatoric and epistemic sources of uncertainty.
To generate predictive intervals suitable for online range management in EVs.

uncertainty_quantification.approach: Bayesian ensembling with a probabilistic output layer
uncertainty_quantification.epistemic_uncertainty_method: A Bayesian ensembling method [pearce2020uncertainty] is adopted and extended to LSTM architectures to capture uncertainty from limited or biased training data.
uncertainty_quantification.aleatoric_uncertainty_method: A probabilistic output layer is incorporated to model uncertainty arising from inherent variability in driving conditions and sensor noise.
uncertainty_quantification.goal: To produce predictive intervals that track error scales for reliable online range management.

deep_read.notes: The paper focuses exclusively on LSTM models, having previously benchmarked them against other architectures (TCNs, Transformers, Random Forest) in prior work [yahyaabadi2025deep].
deep_read.title: Bayesian Uncertainty Quantification with Anchored Ensembles for Robust EV Power Consumption Prediction

problem_setting.name: Open-World Semi-Supervised Learning (Open-World SSL)
problem_setting.description: A generalization of semi-supervised learning and novel class discovery

data_requirements.unlabeled_dataset: Contains instances from both seen and unseen classes

problem_setting.model_task: Either classify instances into previously seen classes or discover new classes and assign instances to them

key_assumptions: New classes can emerge in test data that have never been seen during training
The assumption that training and test data come from the same set of predefined classes rarely holds in real-world scenarios

applications.description: Classifying known cell types and discovering novel cell types in new tissue
Classifying users into predefined interest groups while discovering new interests

robust_semi_supervised_learning.difference: Robust SSL only requires recognizing instances from novel classes as out-of-distribution, while open-world SSL aims to discover individual novel classes and assign instances to them

novel_class_discovery.difference: Novel class discovery assumes unlabeled data is composed only of novel classes, while open-world SSL is more general as instances in unlabeled data can come from both seen and novel classes

limitations_of_existing_approaches: Multi-step approaches combining robust SSL and novel class discovery don't perform well in practice
Treating all classes as 'novel' and then matching them back to seen classes also doesn't work well

figure_references.description: Illustrates the open-world SSL setting, showing that the unlabeled dataset may contain classes not encountered in the labeled set

deep_read.title: Open-World Semi-Supervised Learning

parsed_papers.possible_gap: Based on full paper reading: See deep_read.json for details

deep_read.title: AI-Enabled mm-Waveform Configuration for Autonomous Vehicles With Integrated Communication and Sensing
deep_read.abstract: This paper develops a novel Integrated Communication and Sensing (ICS) framework established on the Markov decision process and deep reinforcement learning. The ICS-AV can adaptively optimize its waveform structure without requiring complete knowledge of the surrounding environment, maximizing sensing and data communication performance under dynamic and uncertain conditions. Simulations show up to 46.26% improvement in joint communication and sensing performance compared to baseline methods.
keywords: Internet of Vehicles (IoV) joint communication and sensing
deep_read.problem: Separate communication and sensing systems in autonomous vehicles lead to increased hardware costs, complexity, and spectrum resource usage. Current vehicular communication standards (C-ITS at 5.9 GHz and DSRC at 5.9 GHz) have data rates (up to 27 Mbps) that don't meet AV requirements.
deep_read.solution: An Integrated Communication and Sensing (ICS) framework based on Markov decision process and deep reinforcement learning to enable adaptive waveform structure optimization.
deep_read.results: Up to 46.26% improvement in joint communication and sensing performance compared to baseline methods.
deep_read.application_domain: Autonomous vehicles (self-driving cars, unmanned aerial vehicles) and Internet of Vehicles (IoV)

process: Input image x₀ is encoded into a latent space using an encoder ℰ
Gaussian noise ε ~ N(0,I) is added to create a noisy latent representation zₜ
The noisy representation is fed through a diffusion model for each possible class condition c
The model predicts the noise ε_θ for each condition
Classification decision is made by selecting the class that minimizes the error between predicted noise and true noise

key_insights: Blood cell morphology is described as complex and requiring expert visual description
The task of morphological characterization is open-ended and lacks definitive ground truth
Simple blood counts of cell class frequencies don't capture the richness of morphological appearances

deep_read.paper_title: Generalized Regularized Evidential Deep Learning Models: Theory and Comprehensive Evaluation
deep_read.problem_statement: Deep Learning models can overfit noise in training data, leading to overconfident predictions. This challenge is compounded in specialized domains where labeled data is limited and costly to obtain.

uncertainty_quantification_approaches.name: Evidential Deep Learning (EDL)
uncertainty_quantification_approaches.description: Combines evidential theory with deep neural architectures to make deterministic neural networks uncertainty-aware
advantages: Successfully extended to classification, regression, meta-learning, and open-set recognition

identified_problems: EDL models achieve competitive performance only on relatively simple learning problems
Performance degrades on complex, large-scale datasets
Sensitive to architecture or hyperparameter changes
Requires careful tuning for stable performance

key_findings.cause: Interaction between non-negative evidence parameterization and specific activation functions that map samples into 'zero-evidence regions'
key_findings.mechanism: EDL couples non-negative evidence parameterization with a KL-based prior that promotes high epistemic uncertainty at class boundaries and in regions far from the training distribution
key_findings.consequence: Commonly used non-negative activations create 'zero-evidence' regions where gradients become extremely small, making evidence updates ineffective

experimental_results.model: Evidential model using ReLU activation and evidential MSE loss
experimental_results.comparison: Nearly 40% lower than a standard softmax model

deep_read.title: Generalized Regularized Evidential Deep Learning Models: Theory and Comprehensive Evaluation

claims.text: The AI Scientist is an AI system that generates novel research ideas, writes code, executes experiments, visualizes results, writes full scientific papers, and runs a simulated review process in an open-ended fashion.
claims.best_result: The AI Scientist can produce papers that exceed the acceptance threshold at a top machine learning conference as judged by the automated reviewer.

claims.text: The AI Scientist-v2 uses a progressive agentic tree-search methodology managed by a dedicated experiment manager agent to autonomously generate scientific manuscripts, eliminating reliance on human-authored code templates and generalizing across diverse machine learning domains, with an enhanced AI reviewer component that integrates a Vision-Language Model feedback loop for iterative refinement of figures.
claims.best_result: One fully AI-generated manuscript exceeded the average human acceptance threshold in a peer-reviewed ICLR workshop, marking the first instance of a fully AI-generated paper successfully navigating peer review.

research_gaps.gap_title: Agentic Synthesis of Novel Uncertainty Methods
research_gaps.text: Current AI agents can generate research papers, but their ability to synthesize domain-specific knowledge for niche technical problems like uncertainty estimation is unproven. There is a gap in understanding how to effectively prompt an agent to combine disparate techniques into a novel, experimentally-validated method for medical imaging.
research_gaps.testable_hypothesis: If an AI agent is provided with a curated knowledge base of uncertainty estimation techniques, then it will be able to generate a novel method that outperforms baselines on a standard medical image classification benchmark.

research_gaps.gap_title: Evaluating the Scientific Validity of Agent-Generated Research
research_gaps.text: Papers like MLE-bench evaluate an agent's ability to complete tasks, but not the scientific rigor or correctness of its output. A critical gap exists in creating a framework to assess whether an agent's proposed uncertainty model is theoretically sound and empirically effective.
research_gaps.testable_hypothesis: If a panel of human experts rates the scientific validity of agent-generated research papers, then these ratings will strongly correlate with the actual performance of the proposed methods on a held-out test set.
research_gaps.suggested_dataset: A curated benchmark of existing uncertainty methods (e.g., from the Uncertainty in Medical Image Analysis workshop).

research_gaps.text: While agents like Reflexion and Self-Refine can use feedback to improve their outputs, their application to the iterative process of scientific discovery is unexplored. The gap is in creating a closed-loop where an agent can diagnose failure in its own uncertainty model and autonomously generate a revised hypothesis.
research_gaps.testable_hypothesis: If an agent is provided with a failure case analysis of its own uncertainty model, then it will be able to iteratively propose a refined model that specifically reduces uncertainty on those identified failure cases.
research_gaps.suggested_dataset: A medical image dataset with curated examples of model failure (e.g., ambiguous pathologies, artifacts).

research_gaps.gap_title: Human-AI Collaborative Frameworks for Clinical Reliability
research_gaps.text: The research focuses on autonomous agents, but medical applications require tight human-in-the-loop collaboration. There is a gap in designing interfaces where a clinician can guide an agent's research on uncertainty estimation, ensuring the resulting methods are clinically relevant and trustworthy.
research_gaps.testable_hypothesis: If a human expert can provide high-level clinical constraints to an agent during its research process, then the final generated uncertainty method will be more aligned with real-world clinical needs and adoption criteria.
research_gaps.suggested_dataset: Any standard medical dataset, but with added metadata on clinical constraints (e.g., inference time, hardware requirements).

research_gaps.text: Agent-driven research typically assumes access to large, centralized datasets, which is not feasible for sensitive medical data. The gap lies in enabling agents to conduct meaningful research on uncertainty estimation within federated or privacy-preserving environments.
research_gaps.testable_hypothesis: If an agent is equipped with tools for federated learning and differential privacy, then it can successfully generate a research paper on uncertainty estimation without ever accessing raw patient data.
research_gaps.suggested_dataset: A federated version of a public medical dataset (e.g., from a simulated hospital consortium).

research_gaps.gap_title: Agentic Discovery of Interpretable Uncertainty Representations
research_gaps.text: Current uncertainty estimation methods often provide a scalar confidence score without explaining the source of uncertainty. The gap is in leveraging AI agents to discover and propose novel methods that not only quantify but also explain *why* a model is uncertain in a specific medical image region.
research_gaps.testable_hypothesis: If an agent is tasked with linking high uncertainty scores to specific visual features or pathologies, then it will be able to generate a novel method that provides both a confidence score and an interpretable explanation for that uncertainty.
research_gaps.suggested_dataset: A dataset with pixel-level or region-of-interest annotations (e.g., from the Pathologist-in-the-Loop dataset).

idea_tree.title: Agentic Uncertainty Synthesis Framework for Medical Image Classification
idea_tree.pitch: An AI agent that synthesizes novel uncertainty estimation methods specifically for medical image classification, addressing the current gap in automated method generation for this domain.
idea_tree.mechanism: The agent will use a multi-step process: 1) Analyze existing uncertainty methods in medical imaging, 2) Generate novel combinations or modifications, 3) Validate through synthetic data, 4) Refine based on performance metrics.
idea_tree.metric: Expected Calibration Error (ECE), Area Under the Reliability Diagram, and Brier Score

idea_tree.title: Scientific Validity Framework for Agent-Generated Uncertainty Methods
idea_tree.pitch: A comprehensive framework to evaluate the scientific validity of uncertainty estimation methods generated by AI agents, ensuring they meet rigorous standards for medical applications.
idea_tree.mechanism: Develop a multi-criteria evaluation system that tests reproducibility, statistical significance, generalizability, and clinical relevance of agent-generated methods through controlled experiments and expert validation.
idea_tree.metric: Reproducibility score, statistical significance p-value, cross-validation accuracy, and expert clinical relevance rating

idea_tree.title: Self-Correcting Uncertainty Agent for Medical Image Classification
idea_tree.pitch: An AI agent that continuously refines uncertainty estimation methods for medical image classification through self-correction based on feedback from validation experiments.
idea_tree.mechanism: The agent will implement a feedback loop: 1) Generate uncertainty method, 2) Test on validation data, 3) Identify failure modes, 4) Generate hypotheses for improvement, 5) Implement and test refinements iteratively.
idea_tree.metric: Improvement in uncertainty quantification metrics over iterations, convergence rate, and final performance

idea_tree.title: Human-AI Collaborative Framework for Reliable Medical Image Classification
idea_tree.pitch: A framework that combines human clinical expertise with AI-generated uncertainty estimation to improve reliability of medical image classification in clinical settings.
idea_tree.mechanism: Develop an interactive system where clinicians can provide feedback on AI uncertainty estimates, which the AI uses to refine its uncertainty methods, creating a human-AI loop for continuous improvement.
idea_tree.metric: Diagnostic accuracy, error reduction rate, clinician satisfaction score, and time to diagnosis

idea_tree.title: AI Scientist for Automated Uncertainty Method Discovery in Medical Imaging
idea_tree.pitch: Applying The AI Scientist framework to automatically discover novel uncertainty estimation methods specifically tailored for medical image classification tasks.
idea_tree.mechanism: Implement The AI Scientist methodology with specialized prompts and evaluation metrics for medical imaging uncertainty, allowing the AI to generate, test, and refine novel uncertainty methods autonomously.
idea_tree.metric: Performance on established uncertainty benchmarks, novelty of generated methods, and reproducibility of results

idea_tree.title: MLE-bench Adaptation for Medical Image Uncertainty Estimation
idea_tree.pitch: Adapting MLE-bench to evaluate AI agents' ability to develop uncertainty estimation methods specifically for medical image classification, addressing the gap in domain-specific evaluation.
idea_tree.mechanism: Create a medical imaging uncertainty benchmark based on MLE-bench methodology, with tasks ranging from basic uncertainty quantification to complex clinical scenarios, specifically designed to test agents' capabilities in this domain.
idea_tree.metric: Task completion rate, uncertainty quantification accuracy, clinical relevance score, and generalization ability

method_matrix.title: The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery
method_matrix.method: The AI Scientist is an AI system that generates novel research ideas, writes code, executes experiments, visualizes results, writes full scientific papers, and runs a simulated review process in an open-ended fashion.
method_matrix.best_result: The AI Scientist can produce papers that exceed the acceptance threshold at a top machine learning conference as judged by the automated reviewer.

key_results.reviewer_performance: near-human performance in evaluating paper scores

method_matrix.title: The AI Scientist-v2: Workshop-Level Automated Scientific Discovery via Agentic Tree Search
method_matrix.method: The AI Scientist-v2 uses a progressive agentic tree-search methodology managed by a dedicated experiment manager agent to autonomously generate scientific manuscripts, eliminating reliance on human-authored code templates and generalizing across diverse machine learning domains, with an enhanced AI reviewer component that integrates a Vision-Language Model feedback loop for iterative refinement of figures.
method_matrix.best_result: One fully AI-generated manuscript exceeded the average human acceptance threshold in a peer-reviewed ICLR workshop, marking the first instance of a fully AI-generated paper successfully navigating peer review.

method_matrix.title: Agent Laboratory: Using LLM Agents as Research Assistants
method_matrix.method: Research paper: Agent Laboratory: Using LLM Agents as Research Assistants

method_matrix.title: MLE-bench: Evaluating Machine Learning Agents on Machine Learning Engineering
method_matrix.method: The paper evaluates ML engineering capabilities of AI agents using Kaggle competitions as tasks, establishing human baselines from Kaggle leaderboards and testing various language models with open-source scaffolds.
datasets_used: Kaggle competitions (diverse set of challenging tasks)
method_matrix.best_result: OpenAI's o1-preview with AIDE scaffolding achieves at least the level of a Kaggle bronze medal in 16.9% of competitions.
limitations: No explicit limitations mentioned in the provided text

method_matrix.title: AIBuildAI-2: Scalable Agentic AI Research and Engineering
method_matrix.method: AIBuildAI-2 is a knowledge-enhanced agent with an external, hierarchical knowledge system that dynamically loads relevant context for AI model building, initialized with web-collected AI development documents and continually evolving through distilled experience from completed tasks.
method_matrix.best_result: First place on MLE-Bench with a 70.7% medal rate
limitations: The performance of AI agents is limited by the static, often outdated, and sparse parametric knowledge of their underlying large language models, particularly on practical AI model engineering know-how
method_matrix.compute_required: Not specified in the provided text

method_matrix.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
method_matrix.method: Uses verbal feedback and memory to help agents improve across trials, motivating explicit failure memory and revision loops.

method_matrix.title: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback
method_matrix.method: Studies iterative generation, feedback, and refinement, providing background for reviewer-style critique and revision policies.

method_matrix.title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
method_matrix.method: Demonstrates open-ended skill acquisition with memory and iterative exploration, relevant to long-running agent loops and durable lessons.

method_matrix.title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
method_matrix.method: Introduces agent evaluation tasks and motivates structured benchmarks for agent behavior rather than isolated text quality.

method_matrix.title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
method_matrix.method: SWE-agent introduces a custom agent-computer interface (ACI) that enables language model agents to autonomously perform software engineering tasks by enhancing their ability to create/edit code files, navigate repositories, and execute tests.
baselines_compared: previous state-of-the-art achieved with non-interactive LMs
